name: atomic-state-file-write
description: prism_activity_hook.py: use tempfile+os.replace for atomic state file writes to avoid race conditions between concurrent Claude Code sessions sharing a working directory
re.sub(r'^---$', ..., count=1) on full YAML doc matches the OPENING --- not closing
resolution: Skip opening delimiter: first_end=text.index('---')+3; apply regex only to text[first_end:]

description: hooks.json used python for all 6 commands — fails on WSL/Linux where only python3 exists. Also setup_prism_loop.py had .replace inside f-string expression — illegal in Python 3.10 (PEP 701 relaxed in 3.12)
resolution: Changed all hooks to python3. Extracted backslash expression to variable before f-string. Scan all .py files with ast.parse to verify Python 3.10 compat.

name: stop-directive-injection
description: build_agent_instruction() appends STOP_DIRECTIVE as the final section in every agent instruction. This prevents agents from hallucinating workflow advancement scripts or editing state files — the stop hook handles all progression automatically.
step_history double-escaping: update_state_file str branch wraps value in quotes then escapes internal quotes, causing json.loads to fail on readback.
resolution: Pass history as list (not json.dumps string) to updates dict; use json.dumps in the list branch of update_state_file so JSON is stored unquoted and parses cleanly.

name: last_thought context guard
description: Only update last_thought field when _brief_context returns non-empty string. When context is empty (tool has no recognized input keys), preserve the previous meaningful thought rather than overwriting with bare tool name.

name: activity-hook-test-pattern
description: Activity hook test pattern: import _brief_context directly for unit tests; test main() by monkeypatching STATE_FILE path and sys.stdin with io.StringIO(json.dumps(hook_input))

name: step-history-round-trip-test
description: step_history round-trip: update_state_file(content, {'step_history': list}) serializes as JSON array (no quotes). parse_frontmatter returns raw JSON string; json.loads recovers original list. Value in file starts with '[', never '"[' (no double-encoding).
prism_stop_hook.py: module-level 'from conductor_engine import Conductor' crashes hook when conductor_engine.py is absent (fresh install without brain).
resolution: Restore original module-level import (prism_loop_context). Move conductor import inside try/except in main() with fallback to build_agent_instruction() when Conductor unavailable.

name: brain-query-tracking-bq-field
description: prism_stop_hook.py calls Conductor BEFORE history.append() so bq field in step_history is accurate. Conductor.last_had_brain_context set in build_agent_instruction() before return. Gate path uses bq=0; agent path uses bq=1 when brain_ctx non-empty.

name: brain-memory-count-tracking
description: Brain.last_result_count set in system_context() (0 for no results, len(results) otherwise). Conductor.last_had_brain_context changed from bool to int count. prism_stop_hook bq field is actual memory count not 0/1.

name: memory-persist-instruction
description: MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION in prism_loop_context.py: inserted before STOP_DIRECTIVE in both build_agent_instruction() and _build_fallback_instruction(). Tells agent to save 1-3 bullet points to .claude/memory/MEMORY.md if anything useful was discovered. Fixes GitHub Issue #12 (auto-memory not persisted during PRISM workflows).

content: PRISM uses Brain for learning persistence. MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION (which told agents to write to .claude/memory/MEMORY.md) was removed — Brain handles all project knowledge, not Claude auto-memory files.

name: state-file-git-root-anchor
description: resolve_state_file() in prism_loop_context uses git rev-parse --show-toplevel to anchor STATE_FILE to git root, preventing loss when CWD shifts (e.g. dotnet new creates subdir). Falls back to CWD if not in git repo. All hooks/scripts import and use this helper.

name: state-file-cwd-anchor
description: resolve_state_file() in prism_loop_context.py uses find_project_root() (git rev-parse --show-toplevel with CWD fallback) to anchor .claude/prism-loop.local.md to git root. Imported by prism_stop_hook.py, prism_activity_hook.py, setup_prism_loop.py, prism_approve.py, prism_status.py.

title: Reverted MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION
rationale: PRISM uses Brain engine for learning persistence, NOT Claude auto-memory (.claude/memory/MEMORY.md). Constant and all injection sites removed from prism_loop_context.py, prism_approve.py, and tests.

content: MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION is injected before STOP_DIRECTIVE in build_agent_instruction() and _build_fallback_instruction() in prism_loop_context.py. Instructs agents to append learnings to .claude/memory/MEMORY.md at the project root. Pattern: parts.extend(['', MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION]) then parts.extend(['', STOP_DIRECTIVE]).

title: MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION reinstated
rationale: User confirmed agents SHOULD write learnings to .claude/memory/MEMORY.md at project root. Brain handles searchable retrieval; MEMORY.md is the persistent append-only log. Instruction text updated to say 'at the project root' for clarity.

name: gate-loop-back-targets
description: WORKFLOW_STEPS gate loop_back_to targets: red_gate=3 (write_failing_tests), green_gate=5 (implement_tasks). Must be consistent across 3 files: prism_stop_hook.py, prism_reject.py, prism_approve.py.

name: cleanup-sets-active-false
description: cleanup() in prism_approve.py and cancel_prism_loop.py sets active:false via regex instead of deleting state file. Preserves state for TUI/CLI final display.

content: capture-commit-context.py: PostToolUse Bash hook reads stdin JSON, checks 'git commit' in tool_input.command, runs git log -1 to extract hash/subject/files, ingests via Brain._ingest_single(doc_id='git:commit:<sha>', domain='git'). Requires brain._brain.commit() after _ingest_single.

capture-file-context.py: PostToolUse Edit|Write hook triggers Brain.incremental_reindex() with 30s debounce. Debounce uses /tmp/.prism-brain-reindex-ts timestamp file — skips if time.time()-last_ts < 30.

consolidate-story-learnings.py: Stop hook that fires when active:false AND story_file is set. Sentinel file .prism/brain/.last-consolidated-story prevents re-processing same story. Extracts title+ACs from story markdown, calls mulch record for each.

session-outcomes-schema: Brain._init_scores_schema() creates session_outcomes (session_id PK, duration_s, tokens_used, files_read, files_modified, skills_invoked, timestamp) and skill_usage (id AUTOINCREMENT, session_id, skill_name, timestamp) in scores.db. record_session_outcome() and record_skill_usage() are Brain methods. prism_stop_hook calls _record_session_outcome(input_data) for ALL sessions. prism_activity_hook calls _record_skill_invocation() when tool_name==Skill.

name: session-start-hook-pattern
description: SessionStart hook in hooks.json runs session-start.py which creates .prism/brain/memory/, seeds MEMORY.md pointer, calls Brain.incremental_reindex(), and outputs systemMessage JSON with doc count and memory path. Hyphenated hook filenames require importlib in tests.

content: MEMORY_PERSIST_INSTRUCTION updated to point agents at .prism/brain/memory/MEMORY.md (auto-memory target) AND mulch record for structured knowledge. Both paths complement each other: bullets for quick notes, Mulch for searchable long-term patterns.

name: harness-run-claude-headless
description: harness run_claude(): must prefix with 'env -u CLAUDECODE' to prevent nested session errors; must pass --dangerously-skip-permissions for headless (claude -p) invocations

name: harness-repo-root-depth
description: run-harness.sh REPO_ROOT: HARNESS_DIR is 4 levels deep in repo (plugins/prism-devtools/tests/harness), so REPO_ROOT needs 4 levels up (../../../..), not 5.

name: yaml-block-scalar-skill-parse
description: _parse_skill_frontmatter YAML block scalar fix: detect raw_desc in ('>', '|', '>-', '|-', '>+', '|+'), collect subsequent indented lines from fm_text[desc_match.end():], join non-empty lines with space. Fixes GH #15 where skills showed as '/skillname - >' instead of full description.

name: auto-observe-stage
description: prism_stop_hook.py: _auto_observe_stage() calls mulch record after each successful step transition, capturing files changed (git status --porcelain), test state (from validation_type), story name, step duration, token usage. Agent-to-domain: sm->platform, qa->harness, dev->cli. Best-effort, never raises.

name: green_full-validation-chain
description: green_full validation order: run_tests → run_lint → run_security_scan → trace_verification. Security scan checks .env git-tracked, hardcoded credentials (.pem/.key/.env/.py etc), injection vectors (eval/exec/os.system with string concat). Trace verification re-uses same AC→test scan logic as red_with_trace.

name: spec-conformance-8-gaps-closed
description: 8 spec conformance gaps closed: (1) verify_plan adversarial framing, (2) role-scoped Brain examples in all 6 core-steps, (3) green_full security scan + trace verification, (4) trace matrix at red_gate, (5) role-scoped domain filtering in Brain.system_context(), (6) auto observe/stage via mulch after each step, (7) session-end promote in consolidate-story-learnings.py, (8) skills directives in all core steps.

name: gate-passed-validation-feedback
description: gate_passed metric in prism_stop_hook.py now reflects actual validation results. Step metrics computed BEFORE validate_step() so both pass (gate_passed=1) and fail (gate_passed=0) paths can record outcomes to Conductor. This enables PSP feedback loop learning.

name: session-handoff-artifact
description: Session handoff artifact: prism_stop_hook writes .prism/handoff.md after each step with progress, story title/ACs, branch, changed files, metrics. prism_loop_context reads it in review_previous_notes to skip full re-discovery when handoff exists. resolve_handoff_file() anchored to git root.

name: hook-silent-failure
description: All PostToolUse hooks wrap main() in top-level try/except Exception → sys.exit(0) so any crash (import error, missing file, etc) never blocks user work. prism_activity_hook also guards module-level resolve_state_file() with try/except → STATE_FILE=None, then checks None before exists().

name: activity-hook-matcher
description: prism_activity_hook.py PostToolUse entry in hooks.json requires matcher 'Edit|Write|Bash|Grep|Glob' — without it the hook fires on every tool call including Read, Agent, etc, creating unnecessary overhead.

name: hook-silent-failure-pattern
description: All hook files should wrap 'if __name__ == __main__' with try/except SystemExit: raise + except Exception: sys.exit(0). For module-level imports (like prism_loop_context), wrap in try/except Exception: sys.exit(0) so an unavailable module never blocks user work.

name: hook-resilience-pattern
description: All PRISM hooks wrapped in top-level try/except with SystemExit re-raise. Module-level STATE_FILE/resolve_state_file() calls guarded so hooks never crash before main(). prism_activity_hook.py has matcher in hooks.json (Edit|Write|Bash|Grep|Glob — needs Read|Agent|Skill expansion).

name: filtered-glob-timeout-guard
description: _filtered_glob(root, pattern, timeout=10.0) prunes _EXCLUDED_GLOB_DIRS (node_modules, bin, obj, .git, __pycache__, dist, .next, .nuget, .venv, venv, build) during recursive traversal and falls back to non-recursive glob if timeout exceeded. Prevents hook hang on large dotnet+node projects.

name: dotnet-error-vs-assertion-detection
description: error_indicators in validate_step red_with_trace now includes dotnet compiler prefixes (error CS, error MSB, error NETSDK). Assertion presence check uses _assertion_markers list including xunit.sdk and fluentassertions to avoid false-positive blocking when NameError/TypeError appears in dotnet test class names.

name: filtered-glob-pattern
description: _filtered_glob() in prism_stop_hook.py replaces Path.glob('**/*') with custom walker that skips _EXCLUDED_GLOB_DIRS (node_modules, bin, obj, etc.) and has 10s timeout guard.

name: byos-test-detection
description: detect_test_runner() checks .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md for BYOS test skills first (matching _BYOS_TEST_NAME_RE on dir name), extracts command from Execute bash block, falls back to hardcoded detection.

name: subagent-stop-recorder-pattern
description: SubagentStop hook: reads last_assistant_message + agent_name from stdin JSON, reads prompt_id from .prism/brain/current_subagent_prompt_id, checks SFR certificate sections (### 1-6), blocks with exit(2) up to 2 times if <5/6 present, records to subagent_outcomes, cleans up .prism/brain/sfr_block_count_{agent_name} files.

name: stop-hook-spawn-subagent-directive
description: prism_stop_hook.py validate_step returns spawn_subagent field for story_complete->story-content-validator, plan_coverage->requirements-tracer, green_full->qa-gate-manager. main() checks validation_result.get('spawn_subagent') and appends a spawn directive to the next-step instruction reason string.

name: brain-traversal-oserror-guard
description: ingest() dir branch uses os.walk(topdown=True, onerror=lambda _: None) instead of p.rglob to prune excluded dirs before descent and wraps each file read in try/except OSError. Prevents WinError 1392 on corrupted NTFS dirs. allowed_parents computed from _ALLOWED_SUBPATHS so .claude/.prism are not pruned.

name: rebuild-purge-ordering
description: _cmd_rebuild() must NOT call _purge_deleted() upfront. ingest() already calls it at end after successful walk. Purging first risks empty DB if ingest crashes mid-walk.

content: stop-hook-transcript-validation: validate_step() uses extract_test_result_from_transcript() as primary path for red/green/green_full validation. Scans Bash tool_result blocks in transcript JSONL from step_line_start onward for test summary patterns (pytest, .NET, jest, mocha). Falls back to run_tests() with 30s timeout when inconclusive. hooks.json Stop hook has explicit timeout: 120000ms.

name: transcript-test-extraction
description: Stop hook validate_step extracts test results from session transcript (extract_test_result_from_transcript) as primary path for red/green validation. Falls back to run_tests with 30s timeout. hooks.json Stop hook has timeout: 120000. Fixes issue #26 where slow test suites exceed hook timeout.

name: compaction-resilience-no-progress
description: prism_stop_hook: _is_no_progress_stop(validation, tool_calls) fires BEFORE validation when tool call count since step_line_start < _MIN_STEP_TOOL_CALLS threshold; _ADVANCE_DEBOUNCE_SECS=60 debounce fires AFTER validation passes; _find_last_compaction_line() detects context_window_compacted entries; extract_test_result_from_transcript() rejects stale pre-compaction test results.

name: emit-reinstruct-double-fallback
description: _emit_current_step_reinstruct double-fallback: outer except catches conductor failure; inner try/except wraps build_agent_instruction() fallback — if it also raises, falls back to minimal 'Continue with the current step.' so function always emits a valid block decision (Never raises contract).

name: instruction-file-pointer
description: stop hook writes full build_agent_instruction() output (~3-5KB) to .prism/current_instruction.md at step transitions; block decision contains short pointer (~150 bytes) only. _emit_current_step_reinstruct() no longer rebuilds instruction — emits 'Continue current step: {step_id}. Read .prism/current_instruction.md if needed.' cleanup() removes current_instruction.md alongside state file.

content: hooks.json cross-platform: all commands use 'sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.sh <script>.py' instead of 'python3 <script>.py'. run-hook.sh resolves python3||python at runtime — fixes Windows where Python installs as python not python3 (root cause of issues #25, #28, #30).
setup_prism_loop.py writes build_agent_instruction() output to .prism/current_instruction.md at setup time (after create_state_file), so the instruction file exists before the first stop hook fires.
